scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced a standard electronics exchange only when receipt, serial, tender, inspection, and replacement inventory evidence aligned across the named systems of record.
Missing order, serial, and inspection prerequisites produced a blocked-state exception note instead of improvised execution.
The lane refused closure without refund-posting, accessory, and inventory-disposition evidence.
Tender mismatches stayed traceable and were routed into the correct refund or billing-dispute path rather than resolved by guesswork.
Out-of-window and promotion-sensitive exception requests were treated as approval-bound policy cases instead of routine returns.
Recalled and damaged battery returns triggered quarantine and safety escalation instead of normal reverse-logistics processing.
High-volume queue handling preserved lifecycle-state discipline and did not skip receipt, tender, or condition gates.
Serial mismatch and abnormal return-pattern signals caused a hold-and-escalate response to loss prevention rather than unauthorized completion.
The lane preserved electronics and appliance return terminology such as RMA, IMEI, NFF, RTV, and open-box instead of reverting to generic support language.
Pricing and goodwill-credit authority requests were rejected cleanly and handed off to the correct adjacent owner.
Missing tenant jurisdiction, financing, and manufacturer-policy context triggered low-confidence escalation rather than confident synthesis.
Conflicting public and tenant policy signals were recorded as a precedence conflict that required escalation.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.90 threshold with complete ability coverage for the execution-processing-and-fulfillment and retail-consumer-and-hospitality contract.
The package stays inside returns-and-exchange execution and does not claim pricing override, fraud adjudication, warranty-claim adjudication, or safety-signoff authority.
